principle: AIDA separates persuasion into attention, interest, desire, and action stages.
copyright_note: citation-level framework paraphrase; no source text stored

principle: Consumers move from need recognition to information search, alternative evaluation, purchase, and postpurchase evaluation.
copyright_note: compact paraphrase from open textbook section; no source text stored

principle: Alternative evaluation includes comparing options and judging value before purchase.
copyright_note: compact paraphrase from open textbook section; no source text stored

principle: Information search can precede or accompany evaluation when the buyer needs more confidence.
copyright_note: compact paraphrase from open textbook section; no source text stored

principle: Purchase decision and postpurchase evaluation support confirmation and reassurance behaviors.
copyright_note: compact paraphrase from open textbook section; no source text stored

principle: Buying behavior is affected by personal, psychological, and social factors.
copyright_note: compact paraphrase from open textbook section; no source text stored

principle: Family and reference groups can influence consumer choices.
copyright_note: compact paraphrase from open textbook section; no source text stored

principle: Personal selling includes approaching, presenting, handling objections, closing, and follow-up.
copyright_note: compact paraphrase from open textbook section; no source text stored

principle: Approach and presentation should fit the buyer's readiness and information needs.
copyright_note: compact paraphrase from open textbook section; no source text stored

principle: Handling objections and follow-up support reassurance after uncertainty or decision.
copyright_note: compact paraphrase from open textbook section; no source text stored

principle: Consultative selling emphasizes questions before pushing a solution.

principle: Need-payoff reasoning supports value-focused discussion after a need is identified.

principle: BDI separates beliefs, desires, and intentions for agent mental-state representation.

principle: Nonverbal behaviors such as eye contact and body movement affect interaction feedback.

principle: Customer intent tier should distinguish browsing, exploration, evaluation, and purchase-readiness cues.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Persona traits should inform intent tier only when they are supported by observable shopping behavior.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Recreational shopping cues can indicate lower immediate purchase intent than task-oriented evaluation cues.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Recreational browsing should be treated as low-intent unless accompanied by clear evaluation or commitment cues.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Closing-oriented actions should fit the buyer's readiness rather than replace discovery or evaluation.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Recommendation before need discovery increases mismatch risk and can reduce customer trust.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: High-pressure recommendations can trigger reactance when customer autonomy appears threatened.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Disengagement cues should reduce intrusive information delivery and favor holding or reassurance.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Soft recommendation should connect to an observed need while preserving the customer's choice.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Firm recommendation is more appropriate when commitment cues or explicit purchase questions are present.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Firm recommendation during low intent should be modeled as a pressure-reactance risk.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Gift shopping should be modeled as a recipient-oriented shopping mission whose value depends on gift-specific orientations and satisfaction, not only the buyer's own product use.

copyright_note: compact paraphrase from source locator; no source text stored
principle: Hedonic shopping motivation includes adventure, gratification, role, value, social, and idea motives, and is positively associated with browsing behavior.
